'''Kyle Feldt''' (born 9 February 1992) is an Australian professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|wg}}er for [[St Helens RFC|St Helens]] in the [[Super League]].

Feldt has played for the [[Prime Minister's XIII]], and was a member of the Cowboys' [[2015 NRL Grand Final]] and [[2016 World Club Challenge]] winning sides.

== Background ==

Feldt was born and raised in [[Townsville]], Queensland, [[Australia]]. He grew up playing rugby union and was pursued by the Brumbies.{{citation needed|date=August 2025}}

He played his junior football for the Norths Thuringowa Devils and attended [[Ignatius Park College]] before being signed by the [[North Queensland Cowboys]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.playersvoice.com.au/kyle-feldt-the-miss-that-haunted-me/|title = 'The miss that haunted me' by Kyle Feldt|date = 28 September 2017}}</ref> He is of Norwegian descent

==Playing career==
===Early career===
Feldt played his senior high-school football for Ignatius Park college in Townsville, playing in the Confraternity winning side with the likes of Michael Morgan, Jay Lobwein, Chris Grivsmuhl and Alex Alisalla, beating both St Brendan's Yepoon and Nudgee College in the process.   In 2008, Feldt was selected for the Queensland under-16 team. In 2009, he played for the Townsville Stingers in the inaugural season of the Mal Meninga Cup. In 2010, Feldt was selected for the Queensland under-18 team.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://18thman.com/representative/under-16s-origin/2008-2/|title=2008|website=18th Man|date=11 June 2014}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://18thman.com/representative/under-18s-origin/2010-2/|title=2010|website=18th Man|date=11 June 2014}}</ref>

From 2010 to 2012, Feldt played for North Queensland's [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|under-20]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://rleague.com/players/Kyle-Feldt_2888/Matches/|title=Kyle Feldt. Matches|access-date=4 February 2016|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20151222124131/http://rleague.com/players/Kyle-Feldt_2888/Matches/|archive-date=22 December 2015 }}</ref> On 30 August 2011, Feldt was named on the interchange bench in the 2011 NYC Team of the Year. He finished his NYC career as the competition's highest ever point scorer, with 570 points.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/toyota-cup-team-of-the-year/tabid/10874/newsid/64496/default.aspx|title=Toyota Cup Team of the Year|publisher=National Rugby League |date=30 August 2011|access-date=22 January 2015|archive-date=11 May 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150511103631/http://www.nrl.com/toyota-cup-team-of-the-year/tabid/10874/newsid/64496/default.aspx|url-status=dead}}</ref>

In the 2011 NYC Grand Final against the [[New Zealand Warriors]], Feldt had the chance to win the premiership for the North Queensland, however he missed a simple conversion on the full-time siren, and the club lost the match in golden point extra time 31–30.<ref>[http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/the-warriors-trump-the-north-queensland-cowboys-after-an-extra-time-field-goal-from-halfback-jordan-meads/story-e6frexnr-1226156104552 The Warriors Trump The North Queensland Cowboys After An Extra Time Field Goal From Halfback Jordan Meads] ''The Daily Telegraph'' {{dead link|date=April 2023}}</ref> On 16 October 2011, Feldt played for the [[Junior Kangaroos]] against the [[Junior Kiwis]], playing on the wing in the 28–16 win at [[Hunter Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/11-nrl-players-feature-in-junior-kangaroos/tabid/10874/newsid/65151/default.aspx|title=11 NRL players feature in Junior Kangaroos|date=6 October 2011|access-date=22 January 2015|archive-date=7 October 2011|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20111007091638/http://www.nrl.com/11-nrl-players-feature-in-junior-kangaroos/tabid/10874/newsid/65151/default.aspx|url-status=dead}}</ref>

On 2 December 2011, Feldt extended his contract with North Queensland to the end of the 2014 season.<ref>{{cite news |title=Feldt signs with Cowboys to 2014 |url=https://www.couriermail.com.au/news/queensland/mackay/sport/feldt-signs-with-cowboys-to-2014/news-story/38b7d1eab992277e2575011e36fcfc42 |access-date=1 April 2023 |work=The Courier Mail |date=2 December 2011}}</ref>

===2013===
In Round 21, Feldt made his NRL debut for the [[North Queensland Cowboys]] against the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]], on the wing in the 30–12 win at [[1300SMILES Stadium]].<ref>{{Cite web | url=https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/meet-nrl-debutant-kyle-feldt-of-the-north-queensland-cowboys/news-story/a59d2181f500dfb8ea08f63a6aa2f895 |title = SuperCoach alert: Meet Kyle Feldt|date = 31 July 2013}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/matt-bowen-stars-as-north-queensland-cowboys-beat-canberra-raiders-30-12/story-e6frexpi-1226630705153|title=Matt Bowen stars as Cowboys Beat Raiders 30–12|website=The Daily Telegraph|location=Sydney|access-date=2015-12-22}}</ref> In round 22 against the [[Penrith Panthers]], he scored his first NRL try in the 36–4 win at [[Penrith Stadium]].<ref>{{Cite web | url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/north-queensland-cowboys-keep-their-season-alive-with-big-win-over-poor-penrith-panthers/story-fn2mcuj6-1226695136466 | title=Cows ride all over poor Penrith| date=11 August 2013}}</ref> After the round 23 match against the [[Gold Coast Titans]], in which he scored two tries in the 22–10 win at [[1300SMILES Stadium]], Feldt injured his ankle at training, ending his debut season.<ref>{{Cite web | url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/north-queensland-cowboys-beat-gold-coast-titans-22-10-in-nrl-round-23/story-fni3fbgz-1226699040076 | title=Spirited Cowboys keep season alive| date=17 August 2013}}</ref><ref>[http://www.townsvillebulletin.com.au/sport/nrl/kyle-feldt-and-wayne-ulugia-set-to-battle-for-remaining-wing-position-at-north-queensland-cowboys/story-fnjfzr6n-1226740612527 Kyle Feldt And Wayne Ulugia Set To Battle For Remaining Wing Position At North Queensland Cowboys]''Towns Ville Bulletin'' {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150122033359/http://www.townsvillebulletin.com.au/sport/nrl/kyle-feldt-and-wayne-ulugia-set-to-battle-for-remaining-wing-position-at-north-queensland-cowboys/story-fnjfzr6n-1226740612527 |date=22 January 2015 }}</ref> Feldt finished his debut year in the NRL with three matches and three tries. He was awarded the North Queensland Rookie of the Year award.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.triplem.com.au/sydney/sport/nrl/news/2013/10/full-list-of-club-award-winners/|title=NRL Club Award Winners|author=Triple M NRL|date=10 October 2013|work=Triple M}}</ref>

===2014===
In February, Feldt played in North Queensland's successful [[2014 Auckland Nines]] squad.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/squads-auckland-nines/|title=SQUADS FOR AUCKLAND NINES|work=Rugby League Week|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160115214258/http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/squads-auckland-nines/|archive-date=15 January 2016}}</ref> He starred in the tournament, finishing as the highest try scorer, alongside [[Parramatta Eels|Parramatta's]] [[Semi Radradra]], with five tries, and was named Breakout Player of the Tournament. On 26 May, Feldt re-signed with North Queensland until the end of 2017.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/kyle-feldt-re-signs-with-cowboys-13488/|title=Kyle Feldt re-signs with Cowboys|publisher=National Rugby League – Zero Tackle}}</ref> After the round 7 loss to the [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles]], Feldt spent time in the [[Queensland Cup]] for the [[Northern Pride RLFC|Northern Pride]] before earning an NRL recall in Round 24 to replace [[Matthew Wright (rugby league)|Matthew Wright]], scoring two tries in the 22–10 win over [[South Sydney Rabbitohs|South Sydney]].<ref>{{cite web| url = http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/a-johnathan-thurstoninspired-north-queensland-claim-a-deserved-2210-win-over-south-sydney/story-fni3fbgz-1227034172262| url-status = dead| archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20140824074323/http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/a-johnathan-thurstoninspired-north-queensland-claim-a-deserved-2210-win-over-south-sydney/story-fni3fbgz-1227034172262| archive-date = 2014-08-24| title = A Johnathan Thurston-inspired North Queensland claim a deserved 22–10 win over South Sydney {{!}} dailytelegraph.com.au}}</ref><ref>{{cite news | url=https://www.townsvillebulletin.com.au/sport/kyle-feldt-determined-to-make-the-best-of-a-bad-situation-for-the-north-queensland-cowboys/news-story/2e43c5833c94c1972bc7b97b4f0fc4de | title=Feldt determined to make best of bad situation | newspaper=Townsville Bulletin | date=24 August 2014 }}</ref> Feldt finished off the [[2014 NRL season|season]] with 7 tries in 10 matches before returning to the Pride where he came off the bench and scored a try in their [[Queensland Cup]] Grand Final win over the [[Brisbane Tigers]].<ref>{{Cite web |title=Pride v Tigers |url=https://www.qrl.com.au/draw/qrl-premiership/2014/round-30/pride-v-tigers/ |access-date=2024-09-23 |website=Queensland Rugby League |language=en}}</ref>

===2015===
In January 2015, Feldt was a member of the newly established [[Queensland Academy of Sport|QAS]] Emerging Maroons squad.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/queensland-name-2015-emerging-squads/tabid/10874/newsid/83307/default.aspx|title=Queensland name 2015 emerging squads|publisher=National Rugby League |date=17 December 2014}}</ref> He again played in the [[2015 NRL Auckland Nines|Auckland Nines]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/cowboys-unveil-2015-nines-squad/|title=COWBOYS UNVEIL 2015 NINES SQUAD|work=Rugby League Week|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160131073732/http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/cowboys-unveil-2015-nines-squad/|archive-date=31 January 2016}}</ref> Feldt played in Round 2 and Round 3 before spending a big part of the season playing for the [[Townsville Blackhawks]] in the [[Queensland Cup]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.townsvillebulletin.com.au/sport/nrl/townsville-blackhawks-coach-kristian-woolf-believes-nrl-return-just-a-matter-of-time-for-kyle-feldt/story-fnjfzr6n-1227326182317|title=Kyle Feldt receives high praise from Blackhawks coach ahead of Residents clash|work=TownsvilleBulletin}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.townsvillebulletin.com.au/sport/local-sport/kyle-feldt-returns-for-blackhawks-ahead-of-burleigh-clash/story-fnjfzs15-1227427743570|title=Kyle Feldt to play against Burleigh after recovering from bicep injury|work=TownsvilleBulletin}}</ref> On 3 May, Feldt represented the [[Queensland Residents rugby league team|Queensland Residents]] against the [[New South Wales Residents rugby league team|New South Wales Residents]], playing at centre and scoring a try in the 36–32 win.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/defending-premiers-dominate-qld-residents-team/tabid/10874/newsid/85717/default.aspx|title=Defending premiers dominate Qld Residents team|publisher=National Rugby League |date=26 April 2015}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/qld-residents-record-thrilling-win/tabid/10874/newsid/85920/default.aspx|title=Qld Residents record thrilling win|publisher=National Rugby League |date=3 May 2015}}</ref> Feldt returned to first grade in Round 24, scoring a [[hat-trick]] of tries in the match against the [[New Zealand Warriors]] at [[Mt Smart Stadium]], which North Queensland won 50–16.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/feldt-takes-his-chance-with-hat-trick/tabid/10874/newsid/89129/default.aspx|title=Feldt takes his chance with hat-trick|publisher=National Rugby League |date=22 August 2015}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/seasons/nrl-2015/round-24/warriors-vs-north-queensland/summary.html|title=NRL 2015 – Round 24|publisher=Rugby League Project|access-date=2015-12-25}}</ref>

On 4 October, in North Queensland's [[2015 NRL Grand Final|Grand Final]] against the [[Brisbane Broncos]], Feldt scored a try shortly after the full-time siren which levelled the scores and gave [[Johnathan Thurston]] a chance to win the match. Feldt scored in the corner after Thurston, pressured by Brisbane front rowers [[Adam Blair]] and [[Sam Thaiday]], was forced to pass to [[Michael Morgan (rugby league born 1991)|Michael Morgan]], who made a break down the right edge and flick passed to Feldt after drawing a defender. Thurston missed the subsequent kick after it hit the upright. Feldt then produced a kick off that forced a mistake from Brisbane halfback [[Ben Hunt (rugby league)|Ben Hunt]] which gave North Queensland the field position required for Thurston to kick the winning field goal to win the match 17–16.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-match-report/nrl-grand-final-2015-north-queensland-cowboys-outgun-brisbane-broncos-to-win-premiership-20151004-gk12is.html|title=NRL Grand Final 2015: North Queensland Cowboys outgun Brisbane Broncos to win premiership|work=The Sydney Morning Herald|date=4 October 2015}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/nrl-grand-final-player-ratings-broncos-v-cowboys-who-starred-and-who-struggled/story-fni3fbgz-1227556818349?sv=d513a72ed0eabb4bbeaad8561926d617|title=NRL Grand Final Player Ratings|work=The Daily Telegraph|location=Sydney|access-date=2015-10-12}}</ref> Feldt finished off his premiership winning [[2015 NRL season]] having played in nine matches, scoring eight tries and kicking one goal.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/Custom/Ni0xNjYwLTQtLS0tLS0yMDU3Ny0tLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0=|title=Custom Match List|publisher=Rugby League Project|date=2015-10-22|access-date=2015-12-25}}</ref>

===2016===
Feldt played in North Queensland's [[2016 NRL Auckland Nines]] squad.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2016/02/03/2016-downer-nrl-auckland-nines-official-squads/|title = 2016 Downer NRL Auckland Nines Official Squads|date = 3 February 2016}}</ref> On 21 February, he was a member of the Cowboys' [[2016 World Club Challenge]] winning side, starting on the wing in the side's 38–4 victory over [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] at [[Headingley Rugby Stadium|Headingley Stadium]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2016/02/22/cowboys-thump-leeds-in-world-club-challenge/|title = Cowboys thump Leeds in World Club Challenge|date = 21 February 2016}}</ref> On 5 May, Feldt was named on the wing in QRL journalist Tony Webeck's Queensland Residents team of the past ten years.<ref>{{Cite news|url=http://www.qrl.com.au/news/2016/05/05/a_residents_team_to_.html|title=A Residents team to match any other|publisher=Queensland Rugby League |date=5 May 2016}}</ref> After showing good form in the early rounds, Feldt was in contention for the [[Queensland rugby league team|Queensland]] [[State of Origin series|State of Origin]] squad but unfortunately after error riddled performances against the [[Brisbane Broncos]] and the [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]], Feldt missed out on a wing spot to [[Corey Oates]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/how-kyle-feldt-let-a-state-of-origin-debut-slip-through-his-fingers-20160523-gp1a12.html|title = How Kyle Feldt let a State of Origin debut slip through his fingers|date = 23 May 2016}}</ref> He finished the [[2016 NRL season|season]] as the Cowboys' top try scorer, with 15 tries in 24 matches.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/Custom/Ni0xNjc3LTQtLS0tLS0yMDU3Ny0tLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0=|title=Custom Match List|publisher=Rugby League Project}}</ref>

===2017===
In January, Feldt was a member of the QAS Emerging Origin squad,<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.qrl.com.au/news/2016/12/09/qas_emerging_origin_.html|title=QAS Emerging Origin Squad|date=8 December 2016}}</ref> and played in the [[2017 NRL Auckland Nines|Auckland Nines]] for the 4th consecutive year.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2017/02/04/2017-downer-nrl-auckland-nines-squads/|title = 2017 Downer NRL Auckland Nines squads|date = 3 February 2017}}</ref> In round 4, he played his 50th NRL game. Soon after, he re-signed with North Queensland until the end of the 2020 season.<ref>{{cite news | url=https://www.townsvillebulletin.com.au/sport/kyle-feldt-resigns-with-cowboys/news-story/d382be46cb64dfcf4ab30ba448a73054 | title=Kyle Feldt re-signs with Cowboys | newspaper=Townsville Bulletin | date=15 May 2017 }}</ref> In Round 16 against the [[Penrith Panthers]], Feldt scored the try of the year by leaping in the air to catch a cross field kick from [[Michael Morgan (rugby league, born 1991)|Michael Morgan]], towering over Penrith winger [[Josh Mansour]] to score in the corner and tie the match at 12–12 all with a conversion to come in the 78th minute. North Queensland backup goal kicker [[Ethan Lowe]] nailed the kick from the sideline to win the match 14–12.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.news.com.au/sport/nrl/kyle-feldt-stuns-with-freakish-try-in-cowboys-thrilling-win-over-penrith/news-story/afc70bf106ee558009986ead4c45f964|title=Kyle Feldt stuns with freakish try in Cowboys' thrilling win over Penrith|date=24 June 2017}}</ref> North Queensland had a finals winning run into the [[2017 NRL Grand Final]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/finals/nrl-2017-cowboys-winger-kyle-feldt-fears-goldenpoint-grand-final-finish/news-story/b70d8a49693466465db825c7c862031e|title=NRL 2017: Cowboys winger Kyle Feldt fears golden-point grand final finish|date=30 September 2017}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://thenewdaily.com.au/sport/rugby-league/2017/09/23/cowboys-beat-roosters-and-odds/|title = Cowboys beat Roosters – and the odds|date = 23 September 2017}}</ref> Feldt started on the wing in the Grand Final against the [[Melbourne Storm]] in which they lost convincingly 34–6.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.couriermail.com.au/sport/nrl/nrl-grand-final-2017-melbourne-storm-defeat-north-queensland-346/news-story/778a6b4e18e29f8e15fd2ae3ee24f2f1|access-date=1 April 2023|website=The Courier Mail|title=NRL Grand Final 2017: Melbourne Storm defeat North Queensland 34–6|date=1 October 2017|author=Peter Badel}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.news.com.au/sport/nrl/nrl-grand-final-player-ratings-who-starred-and-who-stunk/news-story/7e5f5e5b44c22eea60a2d78ccd0c06ab|title = NRL Grand Final player ratings: Who starred and who stunk|date = October 2017}}</ref> For the second straight season, Feldt finished the [[2017 NRL season|season]] as North Queensland's top try scorer with 15 tries in 28 matches.{{citation needed|date=August 2025}}

===2018===

On 23 January 2018, Feldt starred in the testimonial match for [[Johnathan Thurston]] and [[Cameron Smith (rugby league, born 1983)|Cameron Smith]], as North Queensland played their last year grand final opponents the [[Melbourne Storm]]. He scored a hat-trick of tries, the third try coming from a Johnathan Thurston banana kick on the 5th tackle with 18 seconds to go, to steal a 16–14 victory.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/2018/02/23/22/14/thurston-shines-in-wet-testimonial|title = Cowboys pull off miraculous win in final seconds}}</ref> In June, in the lead up to Game 2 of the [[2018 State of Origin series]], Feldt was selected as 19th man for Queensland as cover for the backline.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.qrl.com.au/news/20182/06/19/cowboy-feldt-added-to-camp-maroon/|title = Cowboy Feldt added to Camp Maroon|date = 19 June 2018}}</ref> In Round 24, he scored his second NRL hat-trick in a 44–6 win over [[Parramatta Eels|Parramatta]].{{citation needed|date=August 2025}}

He finished as the club's top try scorer for the third straight season, with 14 tries from 24 games. On 13 September, he was awarded the Coach's Award at the club's presentation ball.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.cowboys.com.au/news/2018/09/13/our-2018-award-winners/|title=Our 2018 award winners!|date=13 September 2018}}</ref>

===2019===

In the 2019 pre-season, Feldt suffered a serious groin injury, which ruled him out for the first eight weeks of the NRL season.<ref>[https://www.couriermail.com.au/sport/nrl/teams/cowboys/cowboys-star-kyle-feldt-reveals-groin-injury-was-like-being-stabbed/news-story/b442a1d4a91d9371f99af3df8705be01 It felt like someone stabbed me] ''The Courier Mail'' (subscription required)</ref> He made his return in North Queensland's Round 9 loss to the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]]. A week later, he played his 100th NRL game in North Queensland's 17–10 win over the [[Parramatta Eels]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.townsvillebulletin.com.au/sport/nrl/cowboys/cowboys-highflyer-kyle-feldt-treating-his-century-clash-just-like-any-other/news-story/1a4f1a291069c4a5d16cc4984298f7aa|title = Subscribe to the Townsville Bulletin}}</ref> Feldt played in all 16 of North Queensland's remaining games following his return from injury, scoring 11 tries and finishing as the club's top try scorer for the fourth consecutive season.{{citation needed|date=August 2025}}

On 11 October, Feldt started on the wing and scored two tries in the [[Prime Minister's XIII]]'s 52–10 win over the [[Fiji Prime Minister's XIII]] side.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2019/10/11/prime-ministers-xiii-in-11-try-romp-over-fiji-counterparts/|title = PM's XIII in 11-try romp over Fiji counterparts|date = 11 October 2019}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.cowboys.com.au/news/2019/09/30/feldt-named-in-pms-xiii-squad/|title = Prime Minster's XIII 2019 squads announced: Wade Graham, Karina Brown to lead youthful side – Cowboys|date = 30 September 2019}}</ref> On 19 October, Feldt was a member of [[Australian Kangaroos|Australia]]'s [[2019 Rugby League World Cup 9s]] winning squad. He scored four tries in the tournament, including one in Australia's 24–10 final win over [[New Zealand Kiwis|New Zealand]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2019/10/07/six-new-faces-named-in-kangaroos-squad/|title=Six new faces named in Kangaroos squad|date=7 October 2019}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2019/10/19/world-cup-9s-live-saturday-draw-teams-results--highlights/|title = Kangaroos take title as Generation Next arrives in style|date = 19 October 2019}}</ref>

===2020===
In February, Feldt was a member of North Queensland's [[2020 NRL Nines]] winning squad. He and teammate [[Jason Taumalolo]] became the first players to win two [[NRL Nines]] championships.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.cowboys.com.au/news/2020/02/15/nines-final-team-list-final-v-dragons/|title=Nines final team list: Final v Dragons|date=15 February 2020}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2020/02/16/drinkwater-studdon-named-players-of-the-nrl-nines-tournament/|title = Drinkwater, Studdon named players of Nines tournament|date = 15 February 2020}}</ref> On 3 June, he re-signed with the Cowboys until the end of the 2024 season.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.cowboys.com.au/news/2020/06/03/feldt-wright-re-sign-with-cowboys/|title=Feldt, Wright re-sign with Cowboys|date=3 June 2020|website=NQ Cowboys}}</ref>

Feldt played all 20 games for the North Queensland outfit in 2020, finishing as the club's top try scorer (19) and point scorer (122). For the fifth straight season, he scored more than 10 tries.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/state-of-origin/teams/queensland/nrl-2020-state-of-origin-queensland-maroons-valentine-holmes-kyle-feldt-kevin-walters-backline/news-story/703d5efd89531be7c9d09aa7c72b2bdd|title=Queensland Maroons dealt double blow as backline stocks plummet with Feldt, Holmes sidelined|date=25 September 2020|publisher=Fox Sports|location=Australia}}</ref> In Round 20, he scored his third career hat trick in a 32–16 win over the [[North Queensland Cowboys|Brisbane Broncos]], Feldt was set to be the Top Try scorer in the NRL for 2020 till the next day when [[Alex Johnston (rugby league)|Alex Johnston]] scored five tries for [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]] in their 60–8 thrashing of the Sydney Roosters.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://sports.yahoo.com/broncos-effectively-wooden-spoon-feldt-131529447.html|title=Broncos effectively get wooden spoon after Feldt hat-trick for Cowboys|date=24 September 2020|website=Yahoo}}</ref> On 3 October, he won the North Queensland Member's Player of the Year award for the second time.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.cowboys.com.au/news/2020/10/02/2020-presentation-night-live-updates/|title=2020 Presentation Night: Live Updates|date=2 October 2020|website=NQ Cowboys}}</ref>

===2021===
Feldt played 20 games for North Queensland in the [[2021 NRL season]] and finished as the club's top try scorer with twelve tries.  It was a disappointing season for North Queensland as they finished second last on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2021/10/19/cowboys-2021-season-by-the-numbers2/|title=Cowboys: 2021 season by the numbers|publisher=National Rugby League|date=18 October 2021}}</ref>

===2022===
In round 8 of the [[2022 NRL season]], Feldt scored a [[hat-trick]] in North Queensland's 35–4 victory over [[Parramatta Eels|Parramatta]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2022-parramatta-eels-vs-north-queensland-cowboys-live-stream-live-blog-supercoach-scores-videos-dylan-brown-valetnine-holmes/news-story/2888307b64713c48e9e964c859e7712d/|title=Hat-trick hero Feldt, Drinkwater shine in Cowboys rout as Eels' attack deserts them: 3 Big Hits|publisher=Fox Sports|location=Australia| date=30 April 2022 }}</ref>
In round 11, Feldt was taken from the field during North Queensland's victory against [[Melbourne Storm|Melbourne]] after being hit in a cannonball tackle by [[Brandon Smith (rugby league)|Brandon Smith]].  Feldt was later ruled from playing for an indefinite period with a knee injury.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2022-casualty-ward-round-11-injuries-ins-and-outs-return-dates-dale-finucane-sharks-luke-brooks-tigers/news-story/92f575de5af44db8610b6b30565520a7/|title=Raiders star's hamstring blow, Souths lose two players to concussion: NRL Casualty Ward|publisher=Fox Sports|location=Australia|date=22 May 2022 }}</ref>
Feldt played a total of 20 games for North Queensland in 2022 and scored eleven tries as the club finished third on the table after the regular season concluded.  Feldt played in both finals matches which included the clubs upset preliminary final loss to [[Parramatta Eels|Parramatta]] which denied North Queensland a Grand Final spot.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-finals-2022-brad-arthur-mitchell-moses-parramatta-eels-vs-north-queensland-cowboys-preliminary-final/news-story/40c6fab40479312a2dab062fc15ecebc/|title='See it in their eyes': The late-season pact and 'massive sacrifice' behind Eels surge|publisher=Fox Sports|location=Australia|date=23 September 2022 }}</ref>

===2023===
Feldt played 18 games for North Queensland in the [[2023 NRL season]] and scored 13 tries as the club finished 11th on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.sportingnews.com/au/rugby-league/news/nrl-2023-north-queensland-cowboys-season-review/7022566a2d89154cc990318b#:~:text=They%20won%20just%20four%20of,the%20nadir%20of%20their%20efforts./|title=NRL 2023: North Queensland Cowboys season review|website=www.sportingnews.com}}</ref>

===2024===
In round 5 of the [[2024 NRL season]], Feldt became North Queensland's record try scorer during the club's victory over the Gold Coast overtaking [[Matt Bowen]]'s record.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2024/04/08/from-morgo-past-mango-cowboys-legends-pay-tribute-to-record-breaking-feldt/|title=From Morgo, past Mango: Cowboys legends pay tribute to the new king|website=www.nrl.com}}</ref>

In round 8, Feldt was inducted as a life member of the North Queensland Cowboys.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2024-04-23 |title=Cowboys team list: Round 8 v Panthers |url=https://www.cowboys.com.au/news/2024/04/23/cowboys-team-list-round-8-v-panthers-anzac-feldt-townsend--2/ |access-date=2024-09-01 |website=North Queensland Cowboys |language=en}}</ref>

In round 21, Feldt scored a hat-trick in North Queensland's 30-22 victory over Cronulla.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2024-north-queensland-cowboys-vs-cronulla-sharks-scores-live-blog-stats-will-kennedy-replacement-sharks-to-play-two-nrl-debutants-scott-drinkwater/news-story/61e9e11fdb11596c3952af51b4932975/|title=Cowboys snap hoodoo as veteran scores hat-trick to sink Sharks to seventh loss: What we learned|website=www.foxsports.com.au}}</ref> On 26 August, it was the announced that Feldt would depart the club at the end of the season.<ref>{{Cite web |author=Staff Writer |date=2024-08-26 |title=Feldt to depart Cowboys |url=https://www.cowboys.com.au/news/2024/08/26/feldt-to-depart-cowboys-239-/ |access-date=2024-08-26 |website=North Queensland Cowboys |language=en}}</ref>
On the same day, Feldt signed a two-year deal with English side [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]]. In round 26, Feldt scored two tries for North Queensland in their 38-30 victory over Melbourne.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c86l5z1g23po|title=St Helens sign Cowboys winger Feldt from 2025|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
The following week, Feldt scored his 150th try in the NRL as North Queensland defeated Canterbury 44-6.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-live-canterburybankstown-bulldogs-v-north-queensland-cowboys-live-updates-blog-scores-video-stephen-crichton-valentine-holmes-nrl-finals-race/news-story/b199bb0af026495ade8262744103c4b4/|title=Massive defensive contrasts define contest as Cowboys star comes of age: What we learned|website=www.foxsports.com.au}}</ref>

===2025===
In round 1 of the 2025 Super League season, Feldt made his official club debut for St Helens and scored a try in their 82-0 victory over [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cg5y9n6z024o|title=Sailor stars as 15-try St Helens thrash troubled Salford|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
On 18 March, it was announced that Feldt would be ruled out for at least three months with a hand injury.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c0l12xg6l03o|title=St Helens' Feldt out for three months with hand injury|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
In round 14, Feldt scored four tries and kicked three goals in St Helens 46-4 victory over Salford.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c74qpj89x28o|title=Stone accused of 'downing tools' after Saints thrash Salford|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
In round 16, Feldt scored a [[hat-trick]] in St Helens 58-0 victory over Salford.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cly18n44518o|title=St Helens thrash Salford in 11-try demolition|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
In round 26, Feldt came under fire for faking injury during St Helens loss against [[Leigh Leopards|Leigh]]. Feldt went down claiming to be hit in the head with a swinging arm but replays show he was not even touched. Feldt was given a green card by the referee and taken off the field for two minutes. In the wake of the defeat, Feldt was accused of simulation by pundits and fans. Feldt played a total of 18 games for St Helens in the 2025 Super League season. He missed their playoff matches due to injury.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/kyle-feldt-embroiled-in-simulation-drama-and-told-to-give-head-a-wobble-after-embarrassing-moment-in-leigh-clash/|title=Watch as Kyle Feldt told to ‘give head a wobble’ after embarrassing ‘simulation’ claim in Leigh clash|website=www.loverugbyleague.com}}</ref>
